rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component selenocyclization of diphenyl diselenide, methacryloyl chloride, and 2-mercaptobenzimidazole to construct selenium-containing benzimidazole-fused thiazinones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: TBPB acts as a redox mediator/initiator and PhSeSePh is oxidized at the anode to generate PhSe radical; cathode reduces protons/water to H2 (with D2O giving D-incorporation in the product).

key_intermediates.name: carbon radical from PhSe• addition to methacryloyl chloride
C-centered radical alpha to carbonyl prior to H/D abstraction

electron_flow: TBPB mediates anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to give PhSe•, which adds to the methacrylamide-type acceptor formed by N-acylation of 2-mercaptobenzimidazole with methacryloyl chloride; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto S and is reduced/protonated (D from D2O) while H2 evolves at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops from 90% to 80% (0.8 equiv) and 54% (1.2 equiv), indicating radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield decreases to 70% (0.8 equiv) and 65% (1.2 equiv).

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield decreases to 67% (0.8 equiv) and 60% (1.2 equiv).

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: 96% D-incorporation at the methyl/CH position of product 6-d (95% yield), indicating proton (deuteron) source from water at the late stage.

notes: CV shows an oxidation feature around 1.327 V when 1a (PhSeSePh) is added to TBPB/Cs2CO3/MeCN, supporting anodic oxidation of the diselenide (likely mediated by TBPB). Radical traps only partially suppress the reaction, suggesting a radical chain or partially caged radical pathway rather than free diffusing radicals exclusively.
